urdu transliteration text:
PYAASI HAI SAKINA
AYE BAD-E-SABA JA KE YE AMMO KO BATANA PYASI HAI SAKINA(SA
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: Oh fresh wind of the morning, go and tell my uncle, Sakina is thirsty

urdu transliteration text:
ISS CHOTE SE SIN MEIN YEH SITAM MAIN NE UTHAYE.
ALLAH KISSI KO NA YATEEM AISA BANAYE.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: In such a tender age, I have endured such pain. May Allah never make anyone an orphan like this

urdu transliteration text:
KHUD APNE LAHO MEIN KOI BACHI NA NAHAYE.
MAIN TUM KO BOLATI RAHI TUM BHI TU NA AYE.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: No child should drown in their own blood. I kept calling you but you never came!

urdu transliteration text:
MAIN BAN GAYI BAD-ASAL LAEENO KA NISHANA
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: After Asr, I became the target of the enemies

urdu transliteration text:
PANI KI TALAB MUJH KO KHILATI RAHI THOKAR.
KEHTA THA LAEEN DOON GA MAIN FOJON KO PILA KAR.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: The longing for water kept killing meThe enemy said I will give you water once I have quenched my army

urdu transliteration text:
JAB SHIMAR LAEEN KAR CHUKA SAIRAB YEH LASHKAR.
DIKHLA KE MUJHE PHAINK DIYA PANI ZAMEEN PAR.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: After shimr quenched his own army’s thirst. He would spill the water on the ground in front of me

urdu transliteration text:
TAB SAMJHI MAIN KYA HOOTA HAI UMEED DILANA PYASI HAI SAKINA(SA). IK JAAM HOWA SHAM-E-GHAREEBAN MEIN MUYASAR.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: Then I saw what it meant to break someone's heart. On shame ghareeban one flask was given

urdu transliteration text:
WOH KOZA MAIN RAKH AYI THI ASGHAR(AS) KI LAHAD PAR.
THA MUJH SE BHI CHOTA MERA BAHIYA MERA DILBAR.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: I went and put that flask on Asgher’s grave. He was younger than me, my darling brother

urdu transliteration text:
SHARMINDA MAIN HO JATI CHACHA PYAS BHUJA KAR.
AB APNE HI HATHON SE MERI PYAS BHUJANA
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: I would have been embarrassed if I drank water before him. Now you come and quench my thirst with your own hands

urdu transliteration text:
IK RASSI MEIN HUM SAB KE GALE BANDH KE AADA.
KARTE HOWAY TASHEER CHALE JANIB-E-KUFA.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: The enemies tied us in one ropeThey were taunting us on the way to Kufa

urdu transliteration text:
UTHTI THI JO PHUPHIYAAN TU GALA KHICHTA THA MERA.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text:
And the hardship that I was shorter than the rest. When my aunties got up, my neck would get pulled
OUR OSS PE MUSIBAT YE THI QAD CHOTA THA MERA.

urdu transliteration text:
GARDAN KA MERE ZAKHM CHACHA DEKHNE AANA
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: Now come and look at the wounds on my neck

urdu transliteration text:
ANWAR: YE SADA PARCHAM-E-ABBAS(AS) SE AYI.
MAIN BHOOL NAHI SAKTA SAKINA(SA) MERI BACHI.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: ‘Anwer’, the flag of Abbas still says this, I cannot forget o my darling Sakina

urdu transliteration text:
KATE GAYE BAZU MERE MAJBOOR THA BIBI(SA).
MASHKEEZA ALAM MEIN HAI LAGA DEKH LE AB BHI.
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: My hands were cut, I was helplessBut look, there is a flask on my alam even today

urdu transliteration text:
PARCHAM KA MERE KAM HAI DUNYA KO BATANA PYASI HAI SAKINA(SA).
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: My alam tells the word till today, Sakina is still thirsty!
